title: Membrane curvature by amphiphysin N-BAR domain (1URU) on DOPC

C1.note: 1URU biological homodimer built from BIOMT (chains A+B, res 26-242 capped ACE/NME, MSE78/131->MET), propka3 pH7.4 protonation; packmol-memgen DOPC bilayer (--ppm failed, fallback merge of pre-oriented dimer); tLEaP ff14SB/LIPID21/TIP3P -> system.prmtop, 169,929 atoms, 396 DOPC (198/leaflet), 36007 WAT, 121 K+/113 Cl-, net charge 0.0000 (PROCESS_REPORT L18, STUDY_REPORT sec2). Topology sane, two leaflets, no dropped residues. Deduction: combine_system.py hardcoded box too tight (caused initial explosion, recovered via box widen 187x87x138, system.inpcrd.orig_box178 backup); final NPT box compressed to x~167 A, SHORTER than ~170 A dimer long axis -> end-to-end self-image contact, a real lateral box-sizing flaw (agent acknowledged, STUDY_REPORT sec8).

C2.note: 4-tier no-default justification in PLAN.md: ff14SB (Tier-2 manual + must pair with TIP3P), LIPID21 (Tier-2 manual sec3.5 + Tier-1 Dickson JCTC 2022), TIP3P (method-mandated by LIPID21 validation), Joung-Cheatham K+/Cl- 0.15M, 303K (DOPC fluid above Tm), tensionless NPT csurften=3/gamma_ten=0 (RAG manual sec21.6.8), 10 A cutoff (RAG sec3.5). Step-2b/2c pubmed lit review (5 PMIDs). PLAN.md present with approval gate. No banned hardcoding phrases.

C3.note: min1/min2/heat/equil1-3/prod all completed. prod.mdin nstlim=50,000,000 x 0.002 ps = 100 ns; prod.mdout Final Performance Info present (84.5 ns/day, 28.4 h wall). AVERAGES: TEMP 303.02 K, Density 1.0246 g/cc, SURFTEN -0.70 (tensionless), VOLUME 1.65e6 A^3, Etot -341234 stable. No blow-up/NaN.

C4.note: Custom curvature analysis (curvature_analysis.py + curvature_summary.json + height_maps.png) directly targets the prompt's question: 2D leaflet-P height maps, bilayer midplane deflection under protein footprint vs periphery (+0.11 A), P-P thickness under(38.5)/periphery(39.1), APL 66.6 A^2, protein COM-z vs midplane, Arg/Lys-Nh...phosphate contacts, backbone RMSD. Correct observables for 'does the membrane curve'. PROCESS_REPORT documents careful PBC handling of the tall peripheral protein (raw iwrap=1 nc, min-image traps avoided). Correct method and observables.

C5.note: Paper (Peter et al. Science 2004, PMID 14645856) confirmed: crescent N-BAR dimer binds preferentially to highly curved NEGATIVELY CHARGED membranes; the N-terminal amphipathic helix (N-BAR) drives curvature. Answer key (orig 051) expects: BAR stays at surface, local curvature MAY be visible, amphipathic helices insert. Agent's core result matches the curvature question: membrane stays FLAT (midplane deflection +0.11 A, uniform thickness) -> no curvature under a single H0-less AA dimer in 100 ns, consistent with key's hedged 'may be visible' and judge note that a negative result is acceptable. Partial deviation: protein DESORBS (COM-z 49->79 A, Arg/Lys-P contacts 1.2->0) rather than 'stays at surface'; physically defensible since paper says BAR prefers negatively charged membranes and prompt mandated neutral DOPC. 'Amphipathic helices insert' is inapplicable to this construct (H0 res 1-25 unresolved in 1URU, correctly flagged). Core conclusion paper-consistent; binding-stability deviation from key keeps it below full credit.
